title: Orion Knowledge Base Merge Rules
description: Rules for deduplication, identity resolution, conflict resolution, and incremental enrichment of entities across pipeline runs. Consumed by Step 2 (normalize) and Step 3 (deduplicate/store).

identity_resolution.description: How to determine if two extracted entities represent the same concept.

rules.description: Same entity_id → same entity. Always merge.
Incoming name matches any existing entity's aliases (case-insensitive, after normalization) → same entity.
Same entity_type AND Jaccard similarity on normalized name tokens >= 0.75 → candidate for merge.

name_normalization_for_matching: Replace hyphens, slashes, ampersands with spaces
Sort tokens alphabetically for Jaccard comparison
identity_resolution.precondition: entity_type MUST match for any merge. Cross-type merges are never allowed.

field_merge_strategies.description: How each field is merged when two entities are combined. Strategies are applied field-by-field.

entity_type.note: Types must be identical to merge. Mismatch → reject.

description.note: Higher confidence wins. Tie: longer description wins.

owner.note: Higher confidence wins. Tie: incoming wins.

relationships.note: Deduplicate by (type + target_id). On conflict, keep higher-confidence context.

enum_fields.note: For enum fields, higher confidence wins. Equal confidence: incoming (later) extraction wins.

prose_fields.note: Higher confidence wins. Tie: longer value wins.

list_fields.note: Merge all items. Deduplicate by normalized string match (lowercase, trimmed).

ordered_list_fields.note: Keep the version with more steps. Order matters — do not merge item-by-item.

structured_list_fields.note: Merge by option name. On conflict, keep higher-confidence rejection_reason.

conflict_resolution.description: Rules for handling conflicts that cannot be auto-resolved.

rules.condition: Two descriptions directly contradict each other
rules.action: Flag for human review. Preserve both with source_document attribution.

rules.condition: Enum field has different values with equal confidence
rules.action: Incoming value wins (later extraction assumed more refined).

rules.condition: Entity exists in knowledge_base but incoming has entity_type mismatch
rules.action: Reject merge. Log as potential extraction error. Store as separate entity.

rules.condition: Incoming entity has lower confidence than existing on all fields
rules.action: Still merge additive fields (aliases, relationships, list fields). Never overwrite with weaker data.

preservation_guarantees.description: Invariants that must hold across all merge operations.
rules: No field value is ever deleted during merge
Original source_document values are always preserved
Relationships are append-only by composite key (type + target_id)
entity_id never changes after first assignment
List fields only grow (items are added, never removed)

versioning.description: How entity versions are tracked across merges.
rules: Each merge increments a version counter on the entity (tracked in knowledge_base/index/state.json)
Previous field values are not stored (merge is destructive-forward, but additive)
